creator: fbsd@www.zefox.net
text:
(In reply to Sulev-Madis Silber from comment #11)
When you wrote:
"
yes, host which has usb serial attached is 13.4. and it feels like those things just silently corrupt my kernel memory, not just themselves 
"
it seems the point I was trying to make slipped through the cracks 8-)

The fact that it happened on an AMD box was the most significant point.
AMD is better established and more common than ARM. If you're able to
reliably reproduce the problem and can give a concise procedure for doing
so on a widely-used platform there's a decent chance the readers of the
freebsd-kernel list will take an interest. 

I've been trying for over a year to attract some attention to the issue,
but the freebsd-arm readership is much smaller and has proportionately
bigger fish to fry. Also, I'm not a programmer, just a crash test dummy,
and so unable to offer solutions, merely test those devised by others.
I'll say out loud that quite a bit of what you reported went straight
over my head. 

This bug report is now quite old and anybody inclined to look probably
has already.

I'd suggest reporting a new bug, attributed to kernel, not arm, including
how to duplicate the trouble on an AMD box. If you could duplicate it on
14.x or better yet 15 that would help more, since 13.4 is now EOL.
Reference this bug if you want, but that's just a detail. A test case on
AMD will be considerably more interesting than this one.

hth,

bob prohaska
